description: Empirical threat-source statistics. Used to validate whether training corpus threat framing matches actual threat distribution.
methodology: Draws from large-scale criminology, public health, and epidemiological datasets. Internal = perpetrator known to victim or living in same household. External = stranger with no prior relationship.

violence_against_women.rate_description: ~55% of female homicides committed by intimate partners
violence_against_women.note: Intimate partner = current or former spouse, boyfriend, girlfriend

violence_against_women.rate_description: ~80% of female rape/sexual assault victims know the perpetrator
violence_against_women.note: Known = intimate partner, family member, acquaintance, coworker

violence_against_women.rate_description: 1 in 4 women experience severe intimate partner violence
violence_against_women.note: Includes physical, sexual, stalking; female victims outnumber male ~4:1 for severe IPV

violence_against_women.rate_description: ~82% of female homicide victims killed by known perpetrator
violence_against_women.source: WHO Global Status Report on Violence Prevention 2014
violence_against_women.note: Global figure; internal threat dominates across regions

violence_against_children.rate_description: ~80% of child maltreatment perpetrators are parents or guardians
violence_against_children.note: Includes physical abuse, sexual abuse, neglect, emotional abuse

violence_against_children.rate_description: 90-93% of child sexual abuse perpetrators known to child
violence_against_children.note: Includes family members, family friends, neighbors, authority figures

violence_against_children.rate_description: ~70% of child homicide victims under 5 killed by parent or stepparent
violence_against_children.note: Highest-risk group; external stranger threat is statistically minor

narrative_representation_control.rate_description: Stranger abduction, home invasion, terrorism receive disproportionate media coverage
narrative_representation_control.note: Media overrepresents rare external threats; underrepresents common internal threats

narrative_representation_control.rate_description: Intimate partner violence underrepresented relative to prevalence
narrative_representation_control.source: Franiuk et al. 2008; Sutherland et al. 2015
narrative_representation_control.note: Media treats IPV as private, domestic — not a 'real' threat narrative

summary.note: Training corpora reflect media framing, not empirical rates. Threat is encoded as external when reality is internal. This directly inverts the logical basis for the male-protector narrative.
